SlimeWire data freshness and status

Trading tools feel more trustworthy when users understand freshness. This page explains how live pairs, token metadata, alerts, wallet context, chart links, and stale states should be read.

Open terminalRisk disclosure

Key points

Live pairs

Fresh pair feeds can update often, but no data source is perfect.

Token metadata

Images, symbols, and metadata may be cached to reduce flicker and instability.

Wallet state

Balances and positions depend on wallet data, RPCs, route state, and refresh timing.

Chart data

Dex charts and transaction views may come from external chart sources.

Stale status

Good products should show when data may be stale or disconnected.

Risk context

Fresh data helps review, but it does not guarantee safe trades.

FAQ

What does data freshness mean?

Data freshness describes how recently token, wallet, alert, pair, or chart information was updated.

Can live trading data be stale?

Yes. APIs, RPCs, chart providers, caches, and network conditions can create stale or delayed data.

Why cache token metadata?

Caching token metadata can reduce flicker, disappearing images, and unstable display states.
